2.q: ETH promotes the growth of alfalfa in saline condition by reducing the expression of MsACO and MsERF8 genes, while increases its germination rate by upregulating MsERF11 gene.

3.q: Sequence similarity analysis clearly suggested that MsERF11 encoded an ethylene response factor protein.
increases its germination rate by upregulating MsERF11 gene.

4.q: Transgenic calli and plant roots overexpressing Alfin1 showed enhanced levels of endogenous MsPRP2 mRNA accumulation.
very low levels of the MsPRP2 transcript could be detected in the shoots of Alfin1-overexpressing plants grown in soil... consistent with Alfin1 overexpression regulating MsPRP2 gene expression

5.q: The recombinant protein binds DNA in a sequence-specific manner, including promoter fragments of the NaCl-inducible gene MsPRP2... Transgenic calli and plant roots overexpressing Alfin1 showed enhanced levels of endogenous MsPRP2 mRNA accumulation.
Alfin1 binds to promoter fragments of the predominantly root-specific and salt-inducible gene, MsPRP2 (Bastola et al. 1998), which contains the Alfin1 DNA-binding consensus sequence GTG(G/N)NG.

7.q: compared with WT control plants, MsGT16-overexpressing Arabidopsis plants displayed stronger growth and higher antioxidant enzyme activity.

9.q: overexpression of MsRCI2B enhances antioxidant defense, reduces oxidative damage... reduced accumulation of malondialdehyde (MDA), hydrogen peroxide (H2O2), and superoxide anion (O2−).

11.q: MsEOBI functions as a transcriptional activator, enhancing salinity tolerance by promoting the biosynthesis of flavonoids and lignin, as well as facilitating the scavenging of reactive oxygen species (ROS).

12.q: overexpression of MsWRKY33 in alfalfa enhanced salt tolerance and conferred significantly elevated ROS scavenging enzyme activity under salt stress.

13.q: Melatonin content in MsSNAT1‐OE#3 and MsSNAT1‐OE#10 was approximately 1.11‐fold and 2‐fold higher than in WT, respectively, indicating that increased MsSNAT1 transcript levels enhance melatonin accumulation.

14.q: Overexpression of MsHPPD was found to significantly increase the level of β-tocotrienol and the total vitamin E content in Arabidopsis seeds.

15.q: the overexpression of MsGME could affect ascorbate biosynthesis in transgenic Arabidopsis; the ascorbic acid levels increased by 1.69- and 1.52-fold in the shoots of transgenic lines.

16.q: OE of MsbZIP14 promotes abscisic acid (ABA) accumulation and enhances saline-alkali tolerance.

20.q: Under 150 mM NaCl on 1/2 MS plates, the survival rate of WT was 42%, whereas the survival rates of OE-2 and OE-4 were only 11% and 18%, respectively, which were significantly lower than that of WT.

23.q: MsHPPD plays a critical role in seed germination via affecting ABA biosynthesis and signaling; ABA content was significantly decreased in imbibed MsHPPD-overexpressing Arabidopsis seeds.

50.q: NCED is a rate-limiting enzyme in ABA biosynthesis; MsbZIP14 directly binds to the promoter of MsNCED3 and activates its expression.

51.q: ZEP catalyzes the conversion of zeaxanthin (Zx) into violaxanthin (Vx) in plastids. This reaction contributes to not only ABA biosynthesis but also the xanthophyll cycle and carotenoid biosynthesis.
